definition: A set of items or amount of material procured or gathered together.
example: In branch-level training, _______ helps explain operational procedures for practical understanding. where a similar notion is compendium.

definition: An enclosure within which workers, prisoners, or soldiers are confined
example: In bank exam practice, _______ is frequently tested in objective questions during assessments. to explain the idea of an enclosure within which workers, prisoners, or soldiers.

definition: One who, or that which, consumes.
example: In financial operations, _______ plays an important role in decision-making in real scenarios. as it is close to related to consumer.

definition: The other party to a financial transaction
example: In financial operations, _______ plays an important role in decision-making in real scenarios. as it is close to related to counterparty.

definition: A person to whom a debt is owed.
example: In bank exam practice, _______ is treated as a core concept for candidates. with focus on a person to whom a debt is owed.

definition: A certificate that certifies an amount of money owed to someone; a certificate of indebtedness.

definition: A person or firm that owes money; one in debt; one who owes a debt
example: In branch-level training, _______ helps explain operational procedures for practical understanding. where a similar notion is related to debtor.

definition: The condition of failing to meet an obligation.
example: To reduce risk, officers must _______ before posting the final report in the branch office. with focus on the condition of failing to meet an obligation.

definition: A reduction in price.
example: During reconciliation, analysts were asked to _______ while checking transaction history for the bank statement. as it is close to rebate.

definition: adj Able to be exchanged.
example: In bank exam practice, _______ is frequently tested in objective questions during assessments. to explain the idea of adj able to be exchanged.

definition: Short for foreign exchange.
example: For professional banking knowledge, _______ is frequently tested in objective questions during assessments. to explain the idea of short for foreign exchange.

definition: Anything that assures a certain outcome.
example: To reduce risk, officers must _______ to maintain financial accuracy during routine operations. to explain the idea of anything that assures a certain outcome.

definition: To bring into debt; to place under obligation.
example: The branch manager instructed staff to _______ for stronger compliance control in daily banking practice. where a similar notion is beholden.

definition: A means of indemnity against a future occurrence of an uncertain event.
example: In financial operations, _______ plays an important role in decision-making in real scenarios. as it is close to indemnity.

definition: One who issues, emits, or publishes.
example: In bank exam practice, _______ is frequently tested in objective questions during assessments. to explain the idea of one who issues, emits, or publishes.

definition: The act of writing a check on an account with insufficient funds, expecting that funds will become available by the time the check clears.
example: In branch-level training, _______ helps explain operational procedures for practical understanding. where a similar notion is related to kiting.

definition: A force compounded by means of a lever rotating around a pivot; see torque.
example: Before final approval, the audit team had to _______ to maintain financial accuracy during routine operations. to explain the idea of a force compounded by means of a lever.
